verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted as a latent complex consisting of the mature TGF-β1 homodimer non-covalently associated with a LAP homodimer, which is in turn covalently (disulfide) linked to LTBP (latent TGF-β binding proteins). LTBP isoforms are themselves covalently crosslinked to ECM, but critically, GARP (LRRC32) and LRRC33 are transmembrane proteins that covalently tether the LAP-TGF-β1 proprotein complex to the outer leaflet of Treg cells, platelets, and other immune/stromal cells via a stable disulfide bond. This constitutes a wash-resistant, covalent attachment to a TM scaffold — qualifying as `stable_surface_attachment`. The surface-displayed latent TGF-β1 on Tregs/platelets is a documented, therapeutically relevant biology (anti-GARP-TGF-β1 antibodies in clinical development target this surface complex). The dominant secreted/matrix-deposited pool is not surface-accessible per se, but the GARP/LRRC33-tethered cell-surface form is a real and targeted entity.
